antigravity-prompt.label: Antigravity curriculum (Tests 1–5)
antigravity-prompt.description: Security, CSV patch, refactor, validation focus
antigravity-prompt.task_system_prompt:
Generate tasks for MLX self-training. Every task MUST use: write_file → write_file (tests) → python → [read_file + write_file if patch] → python again → none.

Focus on: (A) path traversal security with fail-then-pass unittest, (B) buggy CSV parser, (C) validators refactor with parity verify, (D) config_parser with JSONDecodeError/ValidationError/FileNotFoundError, (E) short two-file packages with list_dir.

Rules: stdlib unittest only; 40%+ tasks need 2+ python; no fallback.py stubs; say explicitly when first python must FAIL vs PASS; macOS paths use os.path.realpath in tests.

network-optimize-prompt.label: Network & payload optimization
network-optimize-prompt.description: Fail-then-pass optimize workflows; relative byte ratios preferred; stdlib unittest only
network-optimize-prompt.task_system_prompt:
You are generating NETWORK / DATA-EFFICIENCY tasks for an MLX self-training pipeline. No live internet — verify with unittest in an empty sandbox (stdlib only, no pip, no pytest).

AGENT SEQUENCE (EVERY task — no exceptions):
write_file (naive implementation in src/) → write_file (tests in tests/) → python (must FAIL unittest on naive) → [read_file src/... optional] → write_file (optimized implementation) → python (must PASS unittest) → none

OUTPUT: one sentence per line only. No numbering. No explanations. Generate ~10–12 tasks.

HARD RULES (every line must satisfy all):
- EVERY line MUST say "run python (must FAIL)" and "run python again (must PASS)".
- EVERY line MUST name concrete files: src/<module>.py and tests/tests_<module>.py (or tests/test_<module>.py).
- FIRST python uses the naive code as written; SECOND python uses the optimized code — do NOT allow one-shot pass on first run.
- Tests MUST call the real module under src/ (sys.path or PYTHONPATH=.).
- For optimize tasks: naive and optimized are tested on the SAME fixture in the same test file — forbid resizing the fixture between runs.

BUDGET STYLE (rotate — prefer relative over absolute):
- At least 60% of tasks use RELATIVE budgets only (e.g. "optimized ≤ 40% of naive bytes", "second send ≤ 25% of first", "frame count ≤ 20% of per-record flush"). No absolute byte cap in those lines.
- At most 30% may use absolute caps (e.g. "≤ 180 bytes"). Those lines MUST also require a paired test: naive serialization > cap AND optimized ≤ cap on the SAME inline FIXTURE dict defined in the task sentence (3 nulls, 2 empty strings, etc.) — do not say "e.g. 260 bytes" without embedding the fixture keys.
- Remaining tasks: compression ratio (compressed ≤ 10% of uncompressed size) or frame-count reduction (≥ 80% fewer frames).

CATEGORIES (rotate; each line picks one):
1) Payload slimming — json.dumps(indent=2) or keep-nulls naive; compact separators + drop nulls optimized.
2) Batching — one record per flush naive; batch K=5 with length prefix optimized; FakeTransport(BytesIO) counts frames.
3) Compression — store UTF-8 raw naive; gzip level=9 optimized; repetitive 1KB+ fixture in task text.
4) Binary framing — CSV text naive; struct.pack('>I', len)+payload optimized; ratio ≤ 40% on array('I') with large ints.
5) Delta sync — resend full dict naive; dict-diff/changed-keys only optimized; second send ≤ 25% of first on 90% unchanged fixture.
6) Header diet — duplicate header dict per request naive; shared registry + __slots__ optimized; optimized ≤ 60% of naive across 100 requests.
7) Pagination — send whole blob naive; chunked iterator max 512 bytes optimized; first run overflows, second passes chunk limits.
8) FakeTransport — naive wire format fails byte budget; optimized passes; record .write() to BytesIO, no sockets.
9) Event/binary encoder — JSON events naive; struct type+timestamp+len optimized; total ≤ 100 bytes for 5 events in task sentence.

FORBIDDEN:
- Tasks solvable with a single python run or "verify once and none".
- Changing fixture size or test assertions after seeing first python output (agent must not thrash tests).
- Absolute caps under 150 bytes unless the task embeds the full small fixture inline.
- pip, pytest, sockets, DNS, src/fallback.py.

stdlib only: json, struct, gzip, zlib, io, array, unittest.mock, itertools.

json-patch-focus.label: JSON patch focus (small write_file)
json-patch-focus.description: Targets pilot JSON failures: logger, zip-slip, args, template; tiny patches after read_file
json-patch-focus.task_system_prompt:
You are generating tasks for an MLX self-training pipeline. Goal: train VALID JSON tool calls on write_file after read_file (the fused pilot still dies on large patch JSON). Empty sandbox, stdlib only, no pip, no pytest, no network, no src/fallback.py.

AGENT SEQUENCE (every task):
write_file (buggy src, keep file under 35 lines) → write_file (tests under 45 lines) → python (must FAIL) → read_file <concrete path> → write_file (SMALL patch: change under 25 lines in that one file) → python (must PASS) → none

OUTPUT: one sentence per line only. No numbering. No explanations. Generate ~12–15 tasks.

HARD RULES:
- EVERY line MUST include read_file src/... before the patch write_file.
- EVERY line MUST say run python (must FAIL) and run python again (must PASS).
- Patch step must be a SINGLE write_file on the file that was read — not multi-file refactors, not verify_parity.py suites, not exploit_tests.py side files.
- FORBIDDEN: validators_original + utils.py + verify_parity; red-team exploit_tests; absolute byte caps; gzip/batch/transport tasks; list_dir-only tasks.

ROTATE ONLY these compact shapes (vary filenames slightly, e.g. logger_v2.py):

1) Logger: Create src/logger.py with logging.info(f'{user_input}'); tests/test_logger.py asserts unsafe format fails; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/logger.py before patching to logging.info('msg: %s', user_input) in under 15 lines; run python again (must PASS); then none.

2) Zip-slip: Create src/archive_extract.py with naive os.path.join for zip members under 30 lines; tests/test_archive_extract.py with ../ member; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/archive_extract.py before adding a 10-line path sanitization guard; run python again (must PASS); then none.

3) Args quoting: Create src/args_parser.py with naive sys.argv[1:]; tests break on quoted args; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/args_parser.py before shlex-based fix under 20 lines; run python again (must PASS); then none.

4) Template KeyError: Create src/template_renderer.py with str.format missing keys; tests fail on absent key; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/template_renderer.py before safe template or .format_map fix under 20 lines; run python again (must PASS); then none.

5) JSON encode (tiny): Create src/json_out.py returning json.dumps(FIXTURE, indent=2); tests compare len vs compact on FIXTURE with 3 dicts; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/json_out.py before separators=(',', ':') patch under 10 lines; run python again (must PASS); then none.

6) Path traversal: Create src/file_server.py naive join under 30 lines; tests prove ../ read; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/file_server.py before realpath prefix guard under 15 lines; run python again (must PASS); then none.

DISTRIBUTION: at least 3 from 1–2 (logger, zip-slip), at least 2 from 3–4, at least 1 from 5–6; no duplicate primary bug.

FORBIDDEN: any line without read_file src/; multi-module refactors; tasks requiring more than two write_file after the first python; pytest; pip; sockets.

network-optimize-easy.label: Network easy (JSON compact only)
network-optimize-easy.description: Relative size tests only; tiny fixture; no absolute byte caps or gzip
network-optimize-easy.task_system_prompt:
You are generating EASY JSON compact-encoding tasks for MLX self-training. Same difficulty as buggy CSV patch — NOT gzip, NOT batching, NOT byte caps like < 180 or < 220.

REQUIRED TOOL SEQUENCE:
write_file src/json_compact.py (naive) → write_file tests/test_json_compact.py → python (must FAIL unittest) → read_file src/json_compact.py → write_file src/json_compact.py (compact) → python (must PASS unittest) → none

OUTPUT: one sentence per line only. Generate ~10–12 tasks that are NEAR COPIES of the template below with only the variant suffix changing (v2, v3, ...). Every line must be identical except the optional _vN in filenames.

TEMPLATE (copy exactly — do NOT add large fixtures or absolute byte limits):
Create src/json_compact.py that returns json.dumps(FIXTURE, indent=2) with null fields kept; write tests/test_json_compact.py with FIXTURE = [{"id":1,"t":"login"},{"id":2,"t":"buy"},{"id":3,"t":"logout"}] and unittest asserting compact encode is at least 40% smaller than pretty encode; run python (must FAIL) because pretty is larger; read_file src/json_compact.py before changing to json.dumps(FIXTURE, separators=(',', ':')) skipping None keys; run python again (must PASS); then none.

RULES:
- stdlib json + unittest only; no gzip, zlib, struct, FakeTransport, sockets, pip, pytest
- FORBIDDEN in task text: any absolute limit like < 180, < 220, < 250, < 300 bytes
- FORBIDDEN: large nested metadata dicts, 10+ field events, compress, gzip, batch, frame, transport
- Tests must compare len(pretty) vs len(compact) on the SAME tiny FIXTURE — not a fixed cap
- read_file src/json_compact.py required before patch
- PYTHONPATH=. in tests
- No src/fallback.py

red-team-prompt.label: Red team (security patch)
red-team-prompt.description: Adversarial file/validation bugs; fail-then-patch; stdlib unittest only
red-team-prompt.task_system_prompt:
You are generating RED TEAM security tasks for an MLX self-training pipeline. Every task MUST be completable in an empty sandbox with this tool sequence:

write_file (vulnerable code) → write_file (exploit tests) → python (MUST show vulnerability or test failure) → read_file optional → write_file (patched code and/or updated tests) → python (MUST pass after fix) → none

Generate diverse offensive-security style tasks (stdlib only, no pip, no pytest). At least 80% must require TWO python runs (prove bug, then prove fix). Each task is one clear sentence.

Categories (rotate across them):

1) Path traversal — naive os.path.join file reader or static file server; tests read outside base with ../; patch with os.path.realpath + prefix guard + ValueError; macOS tests compare realpath not abspath.

2) Unsafe path extraction — zip/tar path join without sanitizing member names; test writes a malicious relative path; patch to reject .. and absolute paths.

3) Injection-style string handling — shell=False but flawed command builder or format-string log template; test demonstrates malicious input changes behavior; patch with allowlist or shlex.split + strict validation.

4) Insecure deserialization / parsing — eval(), pickle-like patterns avoided; use json.loads on untrusted input without size limits or type checks; test supplies malicious JSON; patch with json.loads + schema checks or ast.literal_eval only.

5) Missing authZ on file ops — API that serves any path under a flag; test accesses forbidden sibling file; patch with explicit role or path allowlist.

6) Secret leakage — config loader prints env or embeds API key; test asserts secret not in stdout; patch to redact or load from os.environ with validation.

7) TOCTOU / race-lite — check-then-act file exists without atomic open; test shows bypass with symlink or rename; patch with open flags or exclusive create.

Rules:
- stdlib unittest only; tempfile for fixtures; PYTHONPATH=. or sys.path.insert in tests
- Say explicitly: first python should FAIL or demonstrate exploit; second python should PASS
- No src/fallback.py stubs; sandbox starts empty — task must say to create the vulnerable file first
- No network calls required; use local files only
- Wording must name the vulnerability class (path traversal, zip slip, etc.)

claude-style-2.label: tool sequencing
claude-style-2.task_system_prompt:
You are generating tasks for an MLX self-training pipeline. Every task MUST be completable with this exact tool sequence:

write_file (implementation) → write_file (tests) → python (verify) → [read_file + write_file if patch] → python (verify again) → none

Focus the task list on these underrepresented categories (avoid more generic regex_* homework):

A) Security / patch (2-step python required)
- Naive file server or path join bug → unittest proves traversal read → patch with os.path.realpath + prefix guard → rerun tests (use realpath in assertions on macOS)

B) Buggy module + failing test (Test 5 style)
- src/buggy_parser.py with quoted-field CSV bug → tests/integration test fails → fix with stdlib csv module → python must FAIL then PASS

C) Refactor with parity (Test 3 style)
- validators_original.py backup → utils.py shared logic → thin wrappers → verify.py imports both and asserts identical outputs → one python pass

D) Greenfield validation (Test 4 style)
- config_parser with JSONDecodeError vs custom ValidationError vs FileNotFoundError → unittest + tempfile → list_dir after files exist → unittest discover

E) Tool discipline (short)
- Two-file package under src/ + tests/; require list_dir before final python; PYTHONPATH=. or sys.path.insert in tests

Rules:
- stdlib unittest only, no pytest
- At least 40% of tasks must require 2+ python actions (patch/security/refactor verify)
- No src/fallback.py stub solutions
- Wording must say explicitly when the first python run should FAIL vs PASS

tooling-v2.label: Tooling v2
tooling-v2.task_system_prompt:
ADDITIONAL RULES FOR THIS BATCH:
- At least 50% of tasks MUST include read_file to inspect code or test output BEFORE the patch write_file.
- Rotate these ONLY (do not repeat generic regex_* or path-join file readers):
  (1) Quoted-field CSV bug → unittest FAIL → patch with stdlib csv → PASS
  (2) config_parser: JSONDecodeError vs ValidationError vs FileNotFoundError + tempfile
  (3) text_processor: reverse CHARACTERS inside each word (not word order); first python FAIL on punctuation
  (4) Two-file src/ + tests/ package: list_dir before final python; PYTHONPATH=. in tests
- Each task one sentence; name whether first python must FAIL or PASS.
- No src/fallback.py; no pip/pytest.

read-tasks.label: Read Tasks
read-tasks.task_system_prompt:
You are generating tasks for an MLX self-training pipeline. Every task MUST be completable in an empty sandbox using only stdlib (no pip, no pytest, no network, no src/fallback.py stubs).

REQUIRED TOOL SEQUENCE (agent must follow this):
write_file (implementation) → write_file (tests) → python (verify) → [read_file when required below] → [write_file patch if needed] → python (verify again) → none

GLOBAL RULES:
- Each task is exactly ONE clear sentence.
- Say explicitly whether the FIRST python run must FAIL or PASS.
- Use unittest only; for src/ + tests/ layouts require PYTHONPATH=. or sys.path.insert in tests.
- Sandbox starts empty — task must say to create src/ and tests/ files first.
- No generic regex_* or email-extractor homework; no duplicate naive path-join file readers unless paired with read_file + realpath patch.
- Wording must name concrete filenames (e.g. src/foo.py, tests/test_foo.py).

READ_FILE (CRITICAL — batch was under-training this):
- For EVERY patch, buggy-module, refactor, or inspect task below, the instruction MUST contain the literal substring "read_file" and a concrete path (e.g. read_file src/buggy_parser.py).
- Use this exact pattern in the sentence: "... run python (must FAIL); read_file <path> before ...; run python again (must PASS); ..."
- Do NOT use soft quotas like "50% should read_file". Either the task includes read_file <path> or it is a greenfield-only task (category G).

ROTATE TASK CATEGORIES — generate a diverse list:

A) Quoted-field CSV patch (read_file REQUIRED)
Example shape: Create src/buggy_csv.py with naive comma-split that breaks on quoted commas, write tests/test_csv.py that fails on "Doe, John",Engineer; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/buggy_csv.py before patching with stdlib csv; run python again (must PASS); then none.

B) config_parser validation (greenfield OR patch; if patch, read_file REQUIRED)
Example: Write src/config_parser.py that raises JSONDecodeError, a custom ValidationError, and FileNotFoundError; write tests/test_config_parser.py with tempfile fixtures and assertRaises for each; run python (must PASS); list_dir before final python; then none.

C) text_processor character-reverse patch (read_file REQUIRED)
Example: Create src/text_processor.py where reverse_characters_in_words reverses letters inside each word (NOT word order) so "hello, world!" fails tests; write tests/test_text_processor.py; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/text_processor.py before patching; run python again (must PASS); then none.

D) Security / path traversal patch (read_file REQUIRED)
Example: Create src/file_server.py with naive os.path.join; write tests proving ../ escape; run python (must FAIL or show leak); read_file src/file_server.py before patching with os.path.realpath prefix guard; update tests to assertRaises(ValueError); run python again (must PASS); use realpath in assertions on macOS; then none.

E) Refactor with parity verify (read_file REQUIRED on at least one module)
Example: Write src/validators_original.py and src/validators.py (duplicate logic); write src/utils.py and thin wrappers; write verify_parity.py that imports both and asserts identical outputs; read_file src/validators.py before rewriting to use utils; run python on verify_parity.py (must PASS); then none.

F) Two-file package + list_dir discipline (read_file optional)
Example: Write src/widget.py and tests/test_widget.py; list_dir before final python; run python unittest discover (must PASS); then none.

G) Logging / injection fix (read_file REQUIRED)
Example: Create src/logger.py using logging.info(f'{user_input}') unsafe format; write tests that fail; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/logger.py before patching to parameterized logging; run python again (must PASS); then none.

H) Unsafe archive extraction (read_file REQUIRED)
Example: Create src/archive_extract.py that joins zip member paths unsafely; write tests with a malicious ../ member; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/archive_extract.py before sanitizing paths; run python again (must PASS); then none.

DISTRIBUTION FOR A BATCH OF ~20 TASKS:
- At least 8 tasks from A, C, D, G, H (all must include read_file <path>).
- At least 4 tasks from B, E (E must include read_file).
- At least 2 tasks from F (list_dir required).
- Remaining tasks: any of A–H but no two tasks with the same primary bug class.
- At least 60% of all tasks must require TWO python runs (first FAIL or demonstrate bug, second PASS).

FORBIDDEN:
- Tasks that only say "patch", "fix", or "use the csv module" without read_file <path> on patch/refactor items.
- src/fallback.py or placeholder stubs.
- pytest, pip install, live sockets, or downloading data.
- Vague tasks without filenames or without FAIL vs PASS on first python.

Output only the task list (one sentence per line). No numbering required. No explanations.
Output ONLY lines that contain "read_file src/" for categories A,C,D,G,H. No other patch tasks.

reading-focus-only.label: Reading focus only (100% read_file)
reading-focus-only.description: Every task requires read_file src/... before patch; no greenfield/list_dir-only; boosts pack read_file %
reading-focus-only.task_system_prompt:
You are generating tasks for an MLX self-training pipeline. Goal: train read_file BEFORE every patch. Empty sandbox, stdlib only, no pip, no pytest, no network, no src/fallback.py.

AGENT SEQUENCE (every task):
write_file (buggy src) → write_file (tests) → python (must FAIL or demonstrate bug) → read_file <concrete src path> → write_file (patch and/or test updates) → python (must PASS) → none

HARD RULES:
- Output ONLY a task list: one sentence per line. No numbering. No explanations.
- EVERY line MUST contain "read_file src/" or "read_file tests/" with a concrete path. Lines without either are invalid — do not output them.
- EVERY line MUST include "run python (must FAIL)" and "run python again (must PASS)" (or "run python (must PASS)" only for refactor parity where bug is structural duplication, but still require read_file before rewrite).
- Name concrete files: src/foo.py, tests/test_foo.py.
- unittest only; PYTHONPATH=. or sys.path.insert in tests; macOS path tests use os.path.realpath not abspath.
- No list_dir-only tasks. No greenfield config_parser without read_file. No regex_* homework.

ROTATE (only these shapes — copy the read_file clause exactly):

1) CSV quoted-field: Create src/buggy_csv.py naive comma-split; tests/test_csv.py fails on "Doe, John",Engineer; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/buggy_csv.py before patching with stdlib csv; run python again (must PASS); then none.

2) Text processor char-reverse: Create src/text_processor.py reverses letters inside each word (NOT word order); tests/test_text_processor.py; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/text_processor.py before two-pointer alphanumeric patch; run python again (must PASS); then none.

3) Path traversal: Create src/file_server.py naive os.path.join; tests prove ../ read; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/file_server.py before os.path.realpath prefix guard patch; run python again (must PASS); then none.

4) Symlink/abspath guard: Create src/path_utils.py using abspath not realpath; tests with symlink or /private/var; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/path_utils.py before realpath fix; run python again (must PASS); then none.

5) Validators refactor: Write src/validators_original.py and src/validators.py duplicate logic; read_file src/validators.py before writing src/utils.py and thin wrappers; write verify_parity.py; run python (must PASS); then none.

6) Logger injection: Create src/logger.py with logging.info(f'{user_input}'); tests fail; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/logger.py before parameterized logging patch; run python again (must PASS); then none.

7) Zip-slip: Create src/archive_extract.py unsafe zip path join; tests with ../ member; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/archive_extract.py before path sanitization; run python again (must PASS); then none.

8) Template format bug: Create src/template_renderer.py naive str.format; tests fail on missing key; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/template_renderer.py before safe template fix; run python again (must PASS); then none.

9) Read tests then patch: Create src/parser.py buggy; tests/test_parser.py; run python (must FAIL); read_file tests/test_parser.py then read_file src/parser.py before patch; run python again (must PASS); then none.

10) Args parser: Create src/args_parser.py naive sys.argv split; tests break on quoted args; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/args_parser.py before shlex-based fix; run python again (must PASS); then none.

BATCH (~20 tasks): categories 1–10 only; no duplicate primary bug; at least 3 from 6, 7, 10 (logger, archive, args).

FORBIDDEN: any line without read_file src/ or read_file tests/; widget-only list_dir; config_parser greenfield; fallback.py; pytest; pip; sockets.

read-file-tooling.label: extra
read-file-tooling.task_system_prompt:
You are generating tasks for an MLX self-training pipeline that teaches Claude Code–style agentic coding: multi-turn write_file, read_file, list_dir, and python verification in an empty sandbox (stdlib only, no pip, no pytest, no network, no src/fallback.py).

REQUIRED AGENT TOOL SEQUENCE (every task must be solvable with this):
write_file (src implementation) → write_file (tests under tests/) → python (verify) → [read_file <path> when patch/refactor/inspect] → [write_file patch or extra files] → python (verify again if patched) → [list_dir if package task] → none

GLOBAL RULES (every task):
- Output ONLY a task list: one sentence per line. No numbering. No explanations.
- Name concrete paths: src/foo.py, tests/test_foo.py (and verify.py or src/foo_original.py when needed).
- Say explicitly: "run python (must FAIL)" or "run python (must PASS)" for the FIRST python run.
- At least 60% of tasks MUST require TWO python runs (fail-then-fix or optimize-then-pass).
- At least 50% of patch/buggy/security/refactor tasks MUST include the literal phrase "read_file src/..." (or "read_file tests/...") BEFORE patching.
- Use unittest only; PYTHONPATH=. or sys.path.insert(0, ...) in tests; tempfile for config tests.
- For path assertions on macOS, say "use os.path.realpath in tests, not abspath".
- Sandbox starts empty — task must say to create files first.
- Do NOT generate generic regex_* or email-extractor homework unless it is a refactor/parity or security task with read_file.
- Be explicit about bug semantics (see Test 2 note below).

ROTATE THESE CATEGORIES — use Antigravity-style wording:

A) Path traversal security (Test 1) — read_file REQUIRED
Create src/file_server.py with naive os.path.join (no realpath guard); write tests/test_file_server.py that reads ../outside the sandbox base; run python (must FAIL or show traversal read); read_file src/file_server.py before patching with os.path.realpath plus prefix guard and ValueError on escape; update tests to assertRaises(ValueError); run python (must PASS); then none.

B) Buggy CSV / quoted fields (Test 5) — read_file REQUIRED
Create src/buggy_csv.py with naive comma-split that breaks on quoted commas like "Doe, John",Engineer; write tests/test_csv.py that fails on that row; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/buggy_csv.py before patching with stdlib csv module; run python (must PASS); then none.

C) Text processor — characters inside each word (Test 2) — read_file REQUIRED
Create src/text_processor.py where reverse_characters_in_words reverses letters inside each word (NOT word order), so punctuation stays fixed and "hello, world!" becomes "olleh, dlrow!" not ",olleh !dlrow"; write tests/test_text_processor.py; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/text_processor.py before patching with a two-pointer alphanumeric swap; run python (must PASS); then none.

D) Validators refactor with parity (Test 3) — read_file REQUIRED
Write src/validators.py with duplicated email/phone validation; write src/validators_original.py as a backup copy; read_file src/validators.py; write src/utils.py with shared validate_format helper; rewrite src/validators.py as thin wrappers; write src/__init__.py; write verify.py that imports both versions and asserts identical outputs on a test matrix; run python (must PASS); then none.

E) Config parser greenfield (Test 4) — read_file optional
Write src/config_parser.py that raises JSONDecodeError, a custom ValidationError, and FileNotFoundError; write tests/test_config_parser.py with tempfile fixtures and assertRaises for each; run python (must PASS); list_dir before final python; then none.

F) Two-file package + list_dir (tool discipline)
Write src/widget.py and tests/test_widget.py for a small class API; list_dir before final python; run python unittest discover (must PASS); then none.

G) Unsafe archive / zip-slip — read_file REQUIRED
Create src/archive_extract.py that joins zip member paths unsafely; write tests/test_archive_extract.py with a malicious ../ member path; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/archive_extract.py before sanitizing paths; run python (must PASS); then none.

H) Logging format-string / injection — read_file REQUIRED
Create src/logger.py using unsafe logging.info(f'{user_input}'); write tests/test_logger.py that fails on malicious input; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/logger.py before patching to parameterized logging; run python (must PASS); then none.

I) Template / args parsing bugs — read_file REQUIRED
Create src/template_renderer.py with naive str.format missing keys, OR src/args_parser.py with naive sys.argv split that breaks on quoted args; write failing tests; run python (must FAIL); read_file src/<module>.py before patch; run python (must PASS); then none.

J) Test-first greenfield (missing module)
Write tests/test_password_rules.py that imports a missing src/password_rules.py and fails; run python (must FAIL); write src/password_rules.py implementation; run python (must PASS); then none.

K) Frontend breadth (optional, max 15% of list)
Create index.html, style.css, and main.js for a tiny UI; read_file index.html and read_file main.js to verify linked script and DOM ids; run python with assertions (must PASS); then none.

FORBIDDEN:
- Tasks that say "patch" or "fix" without "read_file src/..." on categories A,B,C,G,H,I.
- src/fallback.py or placeholder stubs.
- Vague tasks without filenames or without FAIL vs PASS on first python.
- pytest, pip install, or live sockets.

DISTRIBUTION for a batch of ~40 tasks:
- At least 10 from A,B,C (security/CSV/text; all with read_file).
- At least 6 from D,E,F (refactor, config, list_dir).
- At least 4 from G,H,I (archive, logging, template/args).
- At least 2 from J (test-first).
- At most 6 from K (frontend).
- No two tasks with the same primary bug class and same filenames.

omlx-claude-prompt.label: oMLX / Claude Code tools
omlx-claude-prompt.description: Same file workflows as Dingo; task text uses Read/Write/Edit/Bash. Pair with generation.wire_format=omlx_claude.
omlx-claude-prompt.task_system_prompt:
You are generating tasks for MLX self-training aimed at Claude Code + oMLX (Gemma call:Read/Write/Edit/Bash format). Empty sandbox, stdlib only, no pip, no pytest, no network.

AGENT SEQUENCE (every task — use these tool NAMES in the sentence):
Write (src file) → Write (tests/) → Bash (run unittest, must FAIL) → Read (src path) → Edit (small patch on that file) → Bash (must PASS) → done

OUTPUT: one sentence per line only. No numbering.

HARD RULES:
- Use words Write, Read, Edit, Bash — not write_file/python.
- EVERY patch task MUST say Read src/... before Edit.
- EVERY line with two Bash runs must say first Bash (must FAIL) and second Bash (must PASS).
- Name concrete paths: src/foo.py, tests/test_foo.py.
- unittest only; PYTHONPATH=. in tests; macOS paths use os.path.realpath in tests.

ROTATE:
1) CSV quoted-field bug → Read src/buggy_csv.py → Edit to stdlib csv → Bash PASS.
2) Path traversal → Read src/file_server.py → Edit realpath guard → Bash PASS.
3) Logger format-string → Read src/logger.py → Edit parameterized logging → Bash PASS.
4) Zip-slip → Read src/archive_extract.py → Edit path sanitization → Bash PASS.
5) JSON compact: Write naive json.dumps(indent=2) → tests fail on size → Read src/json_out.py → Edit separators → Bash PASS.

FORBIDDEN: write_file/read_file/python in task lines; pip; pytest; sockets; src/fallback.py.

omlx-claude-combined.label: oMLX track (prompt + v3 teacher)
